svg.title: FaultSense AI — How It Works
svg.desc: Presentation-friendly architecture diagram showing the end-to-end flow from a plain-English question to a diagnosed root cause and fix recommendations. Designed for mixed technical and non-technical audiences.
svg.text: From a plain-English question to a diagnosed root cause and fix — in seconds
Types a question in plain English — no commands, no code
Where engineers type questions and see the full diagnosis — charts, root cause, and step-by-step fix recommendations
Sends the question to the backend
The brain of the system — receives questions, coordinates the AI agents, and sends the answer back to the dashboard
to find the most relevant past cases
Makes it possible to compare against all stored cases
Finds incidents with similar context, even if words differ
Finds exact keyword matches across incident descriptions
Merges both searches to surface the best matches
4 AI agents work in sequence — each agent
hands its findings to the next, like a relay team
Blocks harmful or irrelevant questions before they go further
Retrieves the most relevant past incidents from the knowledge base
Groups incidents by location, network type, and timing to find clusters
Reasons through the evidence to pinpoint what actually went wrong
Produces clear, prioritised action steps the team can act on immediately
Stores questions as searchable AI patterns
Fast full-text lookup across all incidents
Records every step the AI takes
Tracks how long each agent runs
Is the answer faithful to the evidence?
Is it relevant to what was asked?
FaultSense AI · AI-Powered Telecom Fault Intelligence · RAG + Multi-Agent AI + Automated Quality Scoring · 2026
